title: amaryllis multiflora
intro: Amaryllis multiflora, also known as the autumn amaryllis or naked lady, is a bulbous perennial plant with showy, trumpet-shaped flowers that bloom in late summer to early fall. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally employed to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and fever. The health benefits of Amaryllis multiflora include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Amaryllis multiflora is typically prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
